commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Tighten FT verification: only cite translations found via tools

The model was mixing tool search results with its own knowledge,
producing 89.5% of evidence citations without verifiable URLs.
Now: translations_found must come from actual tool results only,
evidence_source is constrained to tool name enum, and prompt
explicitly forbids citing from LLM knowledge.

commit.message:
Fix stale closure bug causing report data loss during photo capture

The camera and upload-complete callbacks captured value/onChange at creation
time. When the user returned from the camera (or an async upload finished),
these callbacks wrote back stale state, overwriting everything else in the
report. Fixed by reading the latest value and onChange from refs.

Fix broken imports from centralized repository resolution module (#8981) ## Summary Fixes import errors from broken module references. Commit 8225e9d46 moved `repo_resolution` from `erk.cli.commands.pr.repo_resolution` to `erk.cli.repo_resolution`, but 13 files still imported from the old location. ## Changes Updated imports in 13 files across three areas: - **PR commands** (2): `rewrite_cmd.py`, `submit_pipeline.py` - **Objective commands** (4): `check_cmd.py`, `close_cmd.py`, `list_cmd.py`, `view_cmd.py` - **Exec scripts** (7): `get_pr_context.py`, `set_pr_description.py`, `update_pr_description.py`, `objective_plan_setup.py`, `objective_save_to_issue.py`, `objective_apply_landed_update.py`, `objective_fetch_context.py` All imports now reference the canonical location: `erk.cli.repo_resolution`. ## Testing - Type checking: ✓ All imports resolved - Unit tests: ✓ 6,141 passed - Lint: ✓ All checks passed - Format: ✓ Import ordering corrected ## Verification The fix restores full CI passage, allowing all erk CLI commands and workflows that depend on repository resolution to function correctly. ## Implementation Plan
Implementation Plan (Plan #8981) The repo_resolution module was moved from erk.cli.commands.pr.repo_resolution to erk.cli.repo_resolution in commit 8225e9d46. Update all 13 files that import from this module to use the new canonical location. Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5
--- To replicate this PR locally, run: ``` erk pr teleport 8981 ```

commit.message:
fix: convert API to Vercel Edge Function for TypeScript import support

Node.js serverless function couldn't bundle TypeScript source imports at
runtime. Edge Function uses esbuild at build time to inline all imports
(DICTIONARY_ENTRIES, search, categoryCounts, wordOfTheDay, findById),
eliminating the need for fs/readFileSync or pre-built JSON files.

commit.message:
fix: use full SDK message parser for mobile reconnect replay (#88)

getAllMessages() used a naive parser that couldn't handle the Claude Code
SDK message format — missing assistant/user messages (nested in
message.content[]), using wrong field names (name vs tool_name), and
hardcoding all tools as in_progress. Replace with convertSDKMessageToParts()
which the live streaming path already uses, and pass the update flag through
so tool results properly merge into pending entries on reconnect.

commit.message:
Add full instructor-side pages: assessments, grades, attendance, forum, notifications

New pages (all scoped to instructor's assigned offerings):
- lesson-materials: link lessons to attachments, edit sort_order
- live-sessions: schedule zoom/meet/teams sessions with status tracking
- assessments: quiz/midterm/final with publish → student notification
- questions: MCQ, true/false, essay, etc. per assessment
- question-options: answer choices for MCQ/true_false questions
- assignments: file/text submissions with late penalty, publish notification
- grades: record raw scores per student per assessment/assignment, notify on release
- gradebook-items: weighted official grade records with override support
- attendance: mark present/absent/late/excused for live sessions or lessons
- announcements: post course announcements with in-app student notifications
- forum-threads: create/pin/lock discussion threads per offering
- forum-posts: reply to threads with soft-delete support
- notifications: send ad-hoc notifications to enrolled students by offering

Updated InstructorLayoutClient.tsx with all new nav items and distinct SVG icons.

Rename annotations (#30)

* Updating CLAUDE.md

* Rename annotations: @RowMapped → @MikromResult, @ParameterMapped → @MikromParameter

annotated class. The new names are clearer, read more naturally, and avoid
potential collisions with other libraries. Also removes unused @QueryResult
and @Parameter annotations.

Propely

commit.message:
Task 68 Phase 4: TransactionManager rollback for ConcurrentGraph

Add TransactionManager::rollback_concurrent(&ConcurrentGraph) which applies
undo records using DashMap interior mutability instead of requiring &mut Graph.

Restore execute_query_with_tx with full undo tracking: ConcurrentSnapshot
captures Arc-cloned property maps before execution (copy-on-write), and
record_undo_diff_concurrent diffs before/after state to populate the undo log.

Request::Rollback now calls rollback_concurrent instead of returning an error.

4 new tests for rollback_concurrent: create node, delete node, property change,
already-finished guard. Total: storage 62 tests, all 984 tests passing.

feat: M³ (MultiModelMode) — inference pipelines + training jobs

New extension at extensions/m3/ with full backend, frontend, and LiteGraph nodes.

Operating — multi-model inference pipelines:
- Pipeline CRUD (create, edit, delete)
- Sequential execution: input → Model A → transform → Model B → output
- Prompt templates with {{input}} placeholder
- Per-step timing and configurable timeout
- Pipeline runner UI with step-by-step output rendering

Training — job management (interface ready, training container future):
- Training job CRUD with method selection (QLoRA/LoRA/Full)
- Hyperparameter configuration (epochs, LR, batch size, LoRA R/Alpha)
- Dataset path validation
- Job lifecycle: created → queued → running → completed/failed
- Adapter management with Ollama conversion placeholder

LiteGraph nodes (6 types):
- m3-model: Ollama model with combo widget, temperature, status dot
- m3-input: pipeline entry point
- m3-output: pipeline exit with result preview
- m3-transform: template, extract_json, summarize, append modes
- m3-router: conditional branching (contains, starts_with, regex)
- m3-training: training job with progress bar and method badge

Frontend:
- M³ tab between CONFIG and FLEET (purple accent #a855f7)
- 6 cards: pipelines, builder, runner, training, new job, adapters
- WS connection at /extensions/m3/ws (1Hz push)
- All _esc(), _fetch(), _wsUrl(), data-* patterns followed

fix: replace broken similar tasks search with FTS5 full-text search (#86)

The find_similar_tasks endpoint was always returning empty arrays because
it used SQL LIKE substring matching with AND logic, required completed
tasks only by default, and treated multi-word input as a single string.

- Add FTS5 virtual table (tasks_fts) with sync triggers for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
- Rewrite search to use word tokenization, prefix matching, and OR logic
- Rank results by relevance using BM25 (title 10x, description 5x, labels 2x)
- Add automatic fallback: completed_only → all statuses → recent tasks
- Change completed_only default to false in MCP schema
- Update triage prompt to pass individual keywords instead of full phrases
- Add 21 unit tests covering keyword matching, filtering, ranking, sync, and edge cases

commit.message:
Smart paste with auto-indentation and fix OpenQuickly force unwrap

Multi-line paste now adjusts indentation to match the cursor's
current line. Introduces ForgeTextView subclass with paste override
that normalizes indent levels. Also fixes force unwrap in
OpenQuicklyWindowController.dismiss().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 12: BC11 hardening — close 8 gaps, add 16 tests, harden API schema

Gaps closed:
- MAJOR-001: datetime.utcnow() → datetime.now(UTC) in session.py
- MAJOR-002: PA-5 single-epoch pearsonr NaN now logged explicitly
- MAJOR-004: harmonics skip count tracked in analysis_metadata
- MAJOR-005: min_sigma_filter now applied when objects carry BC5 scores
- MAJOR-006: cluster p-values Bonferroni-corrected by n_clusters_evaluated
- CRITICAL-001: rendezvous=True emits ADR-045 warning instead of silently no-op
- CRITICAL-002: NonGravitationalParameters.__post_init__ validates ±1e-9 AU/day² bound
- INTEGRATION-001: NetworkRequest constrained via Pydantic Field (1–500 designations, 1–100 years)

Tests: 74 → 90 pass (0 fail); new files: tests/test_pattern_api.py (5 endpoint tests),
11 new tests appended to tests/test_pattern_analysis.py.
OpenAPI spec regenerated with Field constraints.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(test): update broken-views test for idempotent migration

The original migration no longer creates broken views (removed for
idempotency). Update test to verify they're absent instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add stacks infrastructure reconciler with templates and HAProxy migration (#101)

* Add stacks feature design and work plan

Declarative container management system that replaces hardcoded
IApplicationService implementations with data-driven stack definitions.
Supports drift detection, plan/apply semantics, per-service updates,
and HAProxy routing integration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add stack types, definition hash, template engine, and reconciler plan

Implements Jobs 1-3 of the stacks feature:
- Prisma schema with Stack/StackService models and migration
- Shared types and Zod validation schemas for stacks
- Definition hash utility for deterministic config change detection
- Template engine for {{variable}} interpolation in config files
- Stack reconciler plan() method that compares desired vs running state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add stack reconciler apply method and container manager

Implements the apply phase of stack reconciliation: StackContainerManager
handles low-level Docker operations (pull, init containers, config writing,
create/start, healthcheck, stop/remove) and the reconciler's apply() method
orchestrates create/recreate/remove actions with per-service error isolation,
infrastructure provisioning, and DB state updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add StatelessWeb blue-green deployment support to stack reconciler

Extend the stack reconciler to handle StatelessWeb services using
blue-green deployment with HAProxy traffic switching. Adds
StackRoutingManager for HAProxy route/DNS management, connectToNetwork
to StackContainerManager, and widens sourceType to include "stack".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add stack API endpoints with CRUD, plan, apply, status, and history

Implements REST API layer for declarative stack management:
- CRUD endpoints for stacks and individual services
- Plan endpoint to compute reconciliation diff via StackReconciler
- Apply endpoint to execute changes with StackRoutingManager support
- Status endpoint with Docker container state
- History endpoints for applied version snapshots
- Adds stacks permission domain with read/write scopes
- Updates deployment-manager and ai-agent permission presets

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace IApplicationService deploy with stack reconciler for environment start/stop

The stack reconciler is now the sole mechanism for deploying and stopping
containers in environments. startEnvironment() iterates stacks and calls
reconciler.apply(); stopEnvironment() calls reconciler.stopStack(). Deploy
methods removed from MonitoringService and HAProxyService (health/status
methods retained). Tests updated accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add stack seed utilities, plan/apply UI components, and fix build errors

Adds seed functions for monitoring and HAProxy stacks, stack plan/apply
React components with diff view, and TanStack Query hooks. Fixes TypeScript
build errors: removes unused imports/vars in client, uses Prisma.DbNull for
nullable JSON fields in seeds, and adds intermediate unknown casts for
Prisma JsonValue conversions in the reconciler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add versioned built-in stack definitions with auto-sync on startup

Stack definitions (monitoring, haproxy) are now versioned artifacts that
auto-sync to the DB on server startup, allowing existing environments to
receive definition updates when the app is upgraded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add HAProxy legacy-to-stack migration with preview and apply endpoints

Adds migration service to detect legacy HAProxy containers and migrate
them to stack-managed deployments. Includes migration preview UI,
migrate dialog, and bumps haproxy builtin stack to v2 with corrected
network name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add host-level (environment-less) stack support for monitoring

Make environmentId nullable on the Stack model so monitoring can be a
host-scoped singleton using the standard plan/apply lifecycle instead of
the old HostService/MonitoringService system. Add scope field to built-in
stack definitions, split sync into host vs environment paths, update
reconciler/container manager/template engine for optional environment,
refactor monitoring route to use stack-based API, and update all frontend
hooks and pages accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor stacks: extract shared utilities to reduce duplication

Extract common patterns into server/src/services/stacks/utils.ts:
- serializeStack/serializeService (was duplicated in stacks + monitoring routes)
- toServiceCreateInput (was repeated 7 times across routes and builtin-stack-sync)
- isDockerConnectionError (consolidates 3 different error-checking approaches)
- mapContainerStatus, buildContainerMap, groupByProperty, toServiceDefinition
- buildStackTemplateContext, prepareServiceContainer

Also extract stackNameSchema in schemas.ts and runEphemeralContainer in
stack-container-manager.ts. Net result: -228 lines removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix host-level stack template context using wrong project name prefix

For host-level stacks (no environment), buildStackTemplateContext was
falling back to stack.name as environmentName, causing doubled prefixes
like "monitoring-monitoring-telegraf" in config templates. Also fix
monitoring route to use stack name label filter and check container state
directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add StackDeployment history table to record each apply/stop operation

Adds an immutable deployment log so stack operations are traceable beyond
the single latest-snapshot fields on the Stack model. The reconciler now
creates a StackDeployment record after every apply() and stopStack() call,
including unexpected failures. History endpoints return paginated results
from the new table, and triggeredBy (user ID) is passed through from routes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update monitoring.ts

* Fix HAProxy migration failing due to uninitialized DockerExecutorService

The migration created a new DockerExecutorService() but never called
initialize(), so getDockerClient() returned an empty object placeholder
causing "docker.listContainers is not a function" in the stack reconciler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* so much fixing

* Fix HAProxy remediation failing because backends not created before routing rules

The remediation was adding backend switching rules that referenced backends
(e.g. 'test-nginx') without first creating them, causing HAProxy config
validation to reject the transaction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add retry logic for HAProxy DataPlane API initialization after stack apply

The DataPlane API may not be ready immediately after a fresh container is
created. This adds exponential backoff retries to handle that startup delay.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Important adjustments to CLAUDE.md.

* Add generic stack parameters for configurable key-value pairs

Introduces a parameters system on StackDefinitions that allows stacks
to declare typed, validated configuration values with defaults. The
template engine resolves {{params.key}} references across the entire
service definition (config files, ports, env vars, etc.) before
container creation. HAProxy's port customisation migrates from the
bespoke port-utils system to this generic mechanism as the first consumer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* UI improvements: container dashboard cleanup, scroll fix, and minor enhancements

- Update containers page title to "Container Dashboard" with Docker icon
- Remove duplicate CardHeader between tabs and container grid
- Fix scroll-to-top on data refresh by memoizing columns, adding getRowId,
  stabilizing derived state, and using keepPreviousData in query hooks
- Remove environment filters UI (unnecessary with few environments)
- Add "Sync Anyway" button to stacks when already in sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add stack template system with versioned blueprints and draft/publish lifecycle

Move stack definitions from in-code TypeScript objects into a proper template
system stored in the database. Templates act as reusable blueprints with full
version history, while Stacks remain instantiations with specific parameter
values. System templates (HAProxy, monitoring) are seeded on startup; user
templates support a draft/publish workflow for iterative development.

- Add StackTemplate, StackTemplateVersion, StackTemplateService, and
  StackTemplateConfigFile tables with Prisma migration
- Add StackTemplateService class with full CRUD, draft/publish lifecycle,
  system template upsert, and stack instantiation from templates
- Rewrite builtin-stack-sync to create template records then sync stacks
  from them, with backfill for existing stacks
- Add 11 API routes at /api/stack-templates for template management
- Add templateUpdateAvailable drift detection on StackInfo and StackPlan
- Store config files in dedicated table instead of inline JSON

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace builtin TypeScript definitions with JSON template files

Move stack definitions from code (builtin/ folder) to validated JSON data
files (server/templates/). Each template is a directory with a template.json
and referenced config files. A template file loader with Zod validation
discovers, validates, and resolves contentFile references at startup.

- Add template-file-loader.ts with schema validation, contentFile resolution,
  and directory discovery (reusable for user template imports later)
- Create server/templates/haproxy/ with template.json + 3 config files
- Create server/templates/monitoring/ with template.json + 4 config files
- Rewrite builtin-stack-sync.ts to use discoverTemplates() instead of
  importing from builtin/ TypeScript modules
- Remove server/src/services/stacks/builtin/ folder entirely
- Remove server/docker-compose/haproxy/ (configs moved to templates/)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update stack-templates.ts

* Remove Start/Stop Environment buttons and fix tab scroll-to-top issue

Environment start/stop is now managed through stacks, so the buttons,
handlers, port validation, and related imports are removed. Fix tabs
scrolling to top on switch by using forceMount to keep content in the
DOM and adding tabIndex={-1} to prevent focus-driven scrolling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add port and container name conflict detection to stack plan

Detect conflicts with containers outside the stack before deployment:
- Port conflicts: warns when a desired host port is bound by a running
  container from another stack or unmanaged container
- Name conflicts: warns when the desired container name is already taken

Warnings are surfaced in the plan response so users can review before apply.
Same-stack containers are excluded since they will be replaced during deploy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add user docs for Host, Container Logs, and Container Metrics pages

Write three new help articles covering the previously undocumented
routes (/host, /logs, /monitoring). Wire up helpDoc fields in
route-config, register the new applications/monitoring categories
in the doc loader, and update the docs structure plan to reflect
full coverage. Also update API-ROUTES.md with Stacks, Stack Templates,
Monitoring, and new Environment endpoints, and refresh AGENT.md
with stacks architecture and fix stale Jest references.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update help articles to match UI changes on this branch

- environments.md: Remove stale Start/Stop Environment section (buttons
  were removed), remove Port availability section, add Stacks overview
  card and Stacks tab documentation
- viewing-containers.md: Remove Quick filters section (Running Only/All
  buttons were removed from the page header)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add HAProxy Instances help article

Create dedicated documentation for the HAProxy Instances page covering
the instances table, health statuses, remediation, and legacy-to-stack
migration. Update route-config to point /haproxy/instances helpDoc to
the new article instead of the frontends fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix Docker build: update paths after docker-compose → templates migration

The Dockerfile still referenced the deleted server/docker-compose directory.
Also updated the certificate distributor cert path to match the new layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix cert directory to use data/certs instead of templates path

The templates/ directory holds static stack blueprints, not runtime data.
Since HAProxy now uses Docker volumes instead of bind mounts, the host
filesystem cert write is just a local staging fallback — data/certs is
the appropriate location.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix security vulnerabilities in stacks: shell injection, path traversal, bind mounts

- Quote all shell arguments in writeConfigFiles to prevent command injection via file paths
- Validate permissions as octal (3-4 digits) to prevent injection via chmod
- Validate mountPath with safe-character regex to prevent Docker bind injection
- Add path traversal check for contentFile in template loader
- Block bind mounts to sensitive host paths (/, /etc, /proc, /sys, /root, /dev, /boot, docker.sock)
- Add schema-level validation for configFile path and permissions fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace cd server/client/lib commands with npm -w workspace flags

All commands in docs now run from the project root using npm workspaces
(-w server, -w client, -w lib) to avoid working directory confusion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add majority voting for MCQ answer "1" detection

When expected answer is "1", run 3 parallel detections at temperatures
0.3, 0.5, 0.7 and take the majority (2+ of 3 agree). Applies to both
the retry pass during batch marking and remarkSingleQuestion.

"1" is a single vertical stroke that AI frequently misses or misreads,
so multiple attempts with varied temperatures gives more reliable results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Clean UNetSurrogate interface: x_0 only, no sigma/y; FWI buffer auto-refresh

- UNetSurrogate.forward takes x_0 as first arg, sigma/y unused defaults
- All surrogate call sites pass classifier(denoised) directly
- FWI buffer auto-refreshes when exhausted (each sample seen once)

commit.message:
迭代第57轮: 性能优化+通知去重+缓存策略

- Stats API 添加 Cache-Control (s-maxage=300)
- 商品详情页改为 ISR (revalidate=60) 替代 force-dynamic
- 创建 notification-store (Zustand) 去重通知请求
- Header 和 Dashboard 共享通知状态
- Featured reviews API 添加缓存头
- 修复 admin 订单详情 Coupon 字段类型错误
- 新增 admin 订单详情页面和消息加载页
